name: Pupukea / Sunset Beach
desc: World-class surf breaks at your doorstep — Pipeline, Waimea nearby, acre-plus lots, rural luxury.
insider: Winter waves are massive and deadly serious — never turn your back on the ocean at Pipeline, even from the beach
Shark's Cove tide pools in summer are the best free snorkeling on the island — only go when water is flat and clear
Ted's Bakery chocolate haupia cream pie is the real North Shore souvenir — get there before noon or they sell out
Sunset Beach locals surf early morning — by 7am the best surfers have already been out for an hour
Foodland at Pupukea is the only real grocery store on this stretch — it's small, pricey, and always busy
The Banzai Pipeline break is directly off Ehukai Beach Park — the viewing area gets packed during winter surf contests
Pupukea Road climbs the hill to homes on acre-plus lots with ocean views — prices start around $2M and go way up
Waimea Valley botanical garden and cultural site is a peaceful, family-friendly alternative to the intense surf scene
Sunset Beach Elementary is tiny and beloved — the small-school community feel is a huge draw for families with kids
Kelly Slater, John John Florence, and other surf legends have lived along this stretch — you might see them at Foodland
Three Tables beach next to Shark's Cove has great summer snorkeling and is less crowded than its famous neighbor
